text.title: Accessibility Basics Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Accessibility Basics methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Use POUR (Perceivable / Operable / Understandable / Robust) as a categorisation frame for findings — not as a 4-item checklist. Each finding is tagged with its POUR principle.
rule.rationale: Treating POUR as a checklist produces shallow audits because the four principles are categories of failure, not discrete tests.

rule.statement: Every quick-check report pins WCAG version + level (default: 'WCAG 2.2 AA'). Agents and humans MUST cite SC numbers (e.g., 1.4.3).
rule.rationale: Agents conflate WCAG levels and produce mixed-level audits that obscure compliance status; WCAG 2.2 adds four AA criteria not in 2.1.

rule.statement: When reporting scanner findings, the report MUST also state 'scanner catches ≈30% of issues; manual + AT + user testing required'. Standalone scanner reports are forbidden as the only artefact.
rule.rationale: Agents overweight scanner output; the 30% ceiling is the empirical anchor for this rule.

rule.statement: Pre-PR quick-check covers exactly 5 steps: tab through, 200% zoom, alt-text on one image, label on one input, axe DevTools run. Output is one report.
rule.rationale: A bounded 5-step routine is what survives team adoption; longer checklists are skipped.

rule.statement: Form inputs MUST have a visible, programmatically-associated `<label for>`. Placeholder text MUST NOT be used as the label substitute.
rule.rationale: Screen readers announce the associated label on focus; placeholder disappears on input and fails SC 1.4.3 contrast.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-a11y-basics.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
